constraints: Use readable aimed attacks bursts melee flanking and area denial as the baseline
Reserve denser projectile patterns for selected enemies elites events and bosses
Scale difficulty through coordination speed and added layers rather than permanent screen flooding
Preserve readability alongside four player weapons and later Android controls
Place most threats visibly at encounter start
Use clearly telegraphed believable reinforcement entry points
Trigger reinforcements deterministically from objectives time deaths or player position
Never spawn enemies unfairly on top of or invisibly behind the player
Allow skilled players to learn and manipulate timing
Allow retreat and repositioning in ordinary skirmishes
Use lockdowns for major ambushes elites objectives bosses and set pieces
Signal commitment thresholds clearly before entry
Use enemy behaviour rather than universal invisible walls to resist retreat cheese
Let hunters melee units and selected elites pursue across nearby rooms
Keep defensive heavy ranged and objective-bound enemies within authored areas
Protect checkpoints shops and safe spaces
Use flanking suppression grenades or coordinated pushes against doorway abuse
Keep basic enemies mostly direct-aimed
Let specialised gunners snipers launchers and elites lead current velocity
Never perfectly predict sudden boost reversals
Improve prediction and coordination carefully on harder modes without clairvoyance
Allow selected attacks to track during most of the telegraph
Mark the final commitment moment with clear audiovisual feedback
Preserve fair last-second movement or boost reversals
Do not let telegraphs track invisibly through the firing frame
Make ordinary direct attacks collide consistently with appropriate geometry
Allow clearly marked arcing ricocheting piercing cover-destroying or hazard attacks
Use consistent enemy and projectile visual language
Keep cover useful without letting one obstacle solve every encounter
Keep ordinary bullets and beams non-destructible
Allow rockets mines drones slow plasma spheres and similar threats to be shot down
Prevent four continuous weapons from becoming an automatic projectile shield
Defer exact interception tuning to later detailed combat design
Prioritize higher-level discovery questions before more micro-combat rules
Use recurring characters factions discoveries and escalating stakes
Deliver story through concise briefings environmental storytelling dialogue and restrained scenes
Make narrative skippable or accelerated on repeat runs
Support the arcade loop rather than interrupting it
Use a persistent central base or mission interface
Connect authored main missions optional missions shops progression characters and challenges
Allow selected branches in different orders before convergence points
Avoid open-world scope
Keep branching compatible with progression weapon unlocks narrative pacing and deterministic replay

open_questions_added: Player protagonist and mech identity model
World setting tone factions and visual identity
MVP campaign size and number of authored missions
Narrative delivery budget and voice acting boundary
Exact hub services and progression flow
discovery_preference: Prioritize higher-level product campaign world platform scope and MVP-boundary questions before returning to detailed combat implementation rules.

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether the player controls a fixed named protagonist in a signature mech, a customizable pilot identity operating an evolving player-owned mech with recurring authored characters, or an intentionally anonymous mercenary or machine avatar. Preserve the light meaningful narrative, central hub with controlled branching, cosmetic-only monetization, four-weapon mech identity, persistent progression, full offline play, Windows PC first and later Android, future co-op, and the user's request to stay at a higher level. Include emotional attachment, dialogue and story flexibility, cosmetic expression, co-op duplication, production scope, localization and voice implications, player self-insertion, franchise identity, and save/profile structure. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-131 through D-140 and persist at D-140 unless the user changes the batching rule.
